H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong. Highest protocol n in dataset with strongest outcomes — 66% positive. Qual shows fast entrainment (~10 min), with 9/29 loving the music. Activity skew to driving/commuting (5/29) suggests off-label efficacy. AutoGluon predicts 6.488 (lower than observed) — model may underweight overstimulation dynamics. 29 YouTube views highest in library.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: One of the most-used protocols, with two-thirds reporting it helps with overstimulation. Many settled within ten minutes and loved the music. It's doing well but could reach more people.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong. Overstimulation mid-tier achieves 65% positive with settle time 10 minutes dominant. Mix of music opinion (10 liked, 6 neutral). High cross-run drift (0.710) noted — analytical framing evolving. Low YouTube reach (5 views).
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Brings noticeable relief from over-stimulation for most, often within ten minutes. Some find the music just okay while others love it. Low viewership but solid results.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Strong 64% positive with dual-use evidence for sleep (8/23 trying to sleep). Music opinion split (11 liked, 5 neutral). Settle balanced 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts 6.488 — slightly below observed. Only 8 YouTube views.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Popular for sleep, with many reporting it helps within five to ten minutes. The rhythm feels balanced though the music divides opinions. Not many have tried it yet.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. This anxiety protocol outperforms series average by 0.24 points with 63% positive responses. Qualitative data (n=23) shows 10/23 reporting moderate improvement, settle time clustering at 5-10 minutes, and strong music appreciation (10 liked, 6 loved). AutoGluon predicts consistent score (7.596), aligning with observed mean. YouTube reach is minimal (5 views) — efficacy is largely undiscovered.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol lands well — most people feel noticeably calmer within about ten minutes, and they really like the music style. It's not widely discovered yet, but those who try it tend to feel better.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. The second anxiety tier achieves 67% positive with higher consistency than L1 (lower std). Qual data reveals mixed music opinion (9 liked, 5 neutral) but mostly moderate improvement. Settle time peaks at 5 minutes (9/20). AutoGluon aligns at 7.596 predicted. Very low YouTube exposure (6 views).
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Helps with stress for most who try it. Some feel better quickly, others take a bit longer. The rhythm feels comfortable to many. Not reaching many people yet.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Highest variance in anxiety tier suggests responder subgroups. Qual shows 10/20 describing rhythm as "very settling" but 2/20 report no effect. Settle time bimodal (10 min vs full session). AutoGluon stable prediction. Only 4 YouTube views.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: A solid calming choice for anxiety, though a few didn't feel much change. The sound is liked by most, but some needed the full session to settle.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful. Crisis entry shows expected modestly lower mean (0.63 points below series) with 53% positive. Eight respondents report significant improvement, with settle time mostly 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts crisis zone at 7.572 — notably higher than observed, suggesting potential under-performance relative to model expectations.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Works for crisis moments though results vary. About half find real benefit, with relief often coming within five to ten minutes. Not well known yet but has strong potential.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful. Crisis mid-tier shows 55% positive with polarized rhythm opinions (8 very settling, 6 balanced). Qual shows 6/16 neutral music opinion — groove friction with crisis state listeners may be emerging. AutoGluon predicts 7.572 aligned with trend.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed reactions — some loved it, others found it just okay. Over half feel moderate improvement. Could work better with rhythm refinement.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful. Lowest crisis n but fastest settle noted (7/19 within first two minutes), supporting anchor design efficacy. However, 4/19 rate music negatively. AutoGluon top crisis prediction (7.572) diverges from observed — potential latent strength in need of exposure.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Some people settle very quickly in crisis. Many notice real relief despite small sample size. AutoGluon suggests it might be the best for crisis even if not yet proven.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong. Sleep protocol achieves strong 65% positive. Highest music appreciation (10 liked/loved from 13). Settle time fast (5 min mode). High embedding drift (0.654) noted despite score stability — analytical framing showing evolution. Only 4 YouTube views despite strong efficacy.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helps many relax into sleep quickly. Listeners enjoy the rhythm and sound. It's not seen much but works well for those who find it. Analytical models show some shifting patterns recently.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Second sleep tier shows 67% positive but "too busy" rhythm feedback from 3/15 qual respondents. Caregiver skew in listener type (6/15) and longer settle (10-20 min) suggests arc timing mismatch. AutoGluon predicts 7.247 aligning well.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Works for sleep, though some caregivers found the rhythm slightly too active. Generally brings improvement within about ten minutes. Low visibility.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Lowest sleep n (28) with 64% positive. Settle time 5-10 min; rhythm split between balanced (9) and slightly busy (7). Lowest YouTube views (3). No material AutoGluon divergence.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Good sleep results, often kicking in around five minutes. Some find the rhythm slightly busy but still effective for rest. Very few have tried it.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful. First focus tier underperforms siblings (L2, L3) with 56% positive. 9/23 rate rhythm "slightly busy" — possible underperformance driver. Settle time 5 min. AutoGluon predicts 7.513 (not for L1 specifically) but higher than observed suggests untapped potential through entrainment adjustment.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Brings noticeable focus improvement to many, though some find the rhythm slightly busy. Better results may come from longer sessions or smoother flow.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6, confidence=meaningful. Second focus tier achieves 66% positive with therapist activity concentration (4/17). Mixed music opinion (6 neutral). Settle 10 min. AutoGluon clusters with creative protocols. No material divergence.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Effective for focus, with significant improvement reported by many. Links closely to creative flow protocols in how it works. Not standout but solid.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Highest THRIVING mean with 71% positive, 11/29 report significant improvement. Balanced rhythm. AutoGluon predicts 7.513 aligning. 15 YouTube views highest in THRIVING. High std suggests subgroup potential — strong candidate for L4 variant creation.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: The standout for focus — over 70% report real improvement, often significant. Many love both the music and rhythmic qualities. Highest performing in THRIVING.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Creative flow first tier shows modest uplift — 53% positive, 8/21 reporting slight improvement. Qual notes fast settle (6/21 within 2 min) for micro-break use. Low drift (0.718 from prior run). Only 2 YouTube views.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: About half report some creative boost. Many enjoy the music, with some loving it. Works quickly for some within the first minutes. One of the less effective in the set.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Higher mean with lowest variance in Creative Flow tier. 6/15 love music; moderate improvement dominant. Settle around 10 min. AutoGluon clusters semantically with T-FC protocols (0.892 similarity). Only 2 YouTube views.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Those who try it mostly find creative benefit, often loving the musical style. Seems to pair well with focus protocols analytically. Not widely used yet.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing. Highest Creative Flow despite lowest n — 67% positive. AutoGluon predicts 7.513 with cross-bucket potential, suggesting wider utility. 7/25 noticed improvement. High analytical drift (0.684) noted. Only 7 YouTube views.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Early data shows this might be the strongest for creative flow in the series, with most reporting improvement. Worth exploring further despite small sample.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful. Maintenance first tier shows 64% positive with therapy session context heavy (8/23). Settle peaks at 20 min. Mixed music (6 liked, 6 neutral). High drift (0.672) analytical evolution noted. Only 3 YouTube views.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Good balance maintenance for most, used often in therapy contexts. Opinions split on the music — either liked or just neutral. Takes about twenty minutes to fully settle.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Highest variance in Maintenance (std 2.03) with 59% positive. High drift (0.733). AutoGluon predicts 7.250 — above observed, indicating underperformance. Caregiver overlap notable. Cluster with X-ME-L2 (0.902) suggests shared mechanism.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Helps maintain balance for many, though models suggest it might work even better than current scores show. May share qualities with mood protocols unexpectedly.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Development n for Orbit with 71% positive. Settle 5 min dominant but 4/19 report did NOT settle — attention cadence mismatch. Lowest n limits generalization. 3 YouTube views.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Early data looks promising for balance, though some don't fully settle with it. Worth trying when needing maintenance support. Mixed opinions on the rhythm.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. AutoGluon predicts highest cross-bucket score (8.289) but observed 6.74 — largest negative delta in dataset suggests entrainment failure or population skew. 54% positive with 10/22 loving music. Drift (0.656) indicates analytical evolution. Uses 8 YouTube views efficiently given moderate efficacy.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Models rate this as potentially the strongest protocol overall, though current scores don't quite match. Many love the music and find some energy benefits. Design may be underdelivering.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Mood/energy mid-tier achieves 64% positive — strong for ASCENT. 7/15 report significant improvement. AutoGluon predicts 8.289 (same as L1) — above observed. Clusters with T-MB-L2 (0.902) unexpectedly. 8 YouTube views.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Good mood and energy results, with significant improvement for many. Works similarly to some maintenance protocols unexpectedly. More data would help confirm.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Lowest ASCENT positive rate at 56%, with 5/25 reporting no effect. 2 negative music opinions. AutoGluon does not elevate. High drift (0.712) shows evolving pattern. Only 4 YouTube views.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Early mood results are okay but not standout. Some find no effect while others report real energy lift — needs more data to understand the split.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Motivation first tier shows 45% positive — lowest in ASCENT. High variance indicates mixed response. Settle time shows quick responders (4/17 immediate). AutoGluon predicts 7.621 — significant positive delta suggests entrainment design failing to deliver potential. 5 YouTube views.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Some find significant motivational boost, though less than half report clear help. Models predict this should work much better — possible entrainment timing issue.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Lowest ASCENT positive rate at 43% with 5/21 needing 20 min settle, suggesting arc timing mismatch. 9/17 love music despite lower outcomes. AutoGluon predicts 7.621. Semantic cluster with X-ME-L2 (0.892). 7 YouTube views.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Limited data shows mixed motivation results. Some find long settle times — about twenty minutes. Shares hidden qualities with mood protocols that hint at design timing mismatch.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. Unexpected high mean in L3 exceeds siblings despite low n — possible sampling noise or subtype resonance. 62% positive with 8/17 moderate improvement. High std (2.38) flags subgroup potential. AutoGluon aligns at 7.621. 7 YouTube views.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Shows good motivation potential despite small numbers. The highest level in this tier performs better than others, though sample is limited.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Mind clarity first tier at 54% positive. Unusual negative reports (2 made things worse) despite 9 loving music. Qual settle split 8/25 at 10 min. AutoGluon predicts 7.386. Low similarity mean (0.626) isolated in analysis. 8 YouTube views.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Improves mind clarity for many, with strong music appeal. However, a couple report it makes things worse — needs targeted use for right listener type.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Highest cross-run drift (0.631) in entire set — analytical framing highly unstable. Only 2-timepoint data but flags monitoring need. 56% positive with 6 loving music. AutoGluon predicts 7.386. Lowest similarity mean (0.584) makes this protocol analytically distinct. 11 YouTube views.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Shows potential for clarity but behaves differently than other protocols analytics-wise. Highest analytical instability — might work very well for specific listeners.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, confidence=developing. Lowest mean across all protocols with 50% positive distribution. High std suggests responder variability; 4/17 describe rhythm as too busy. AutoGluon predicts 7.386 vs observed 5.7 — largest negative delta. Extreme drift (0.634) confirms unstable analytical framing. Needs redesign or validation before continued use.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Early results vary widely — works well for some but not all clarity needs. The highest level in this set may need redesign or validation. Lowest scoring protocol.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The complete Drum Protocols library achieves consistent mid-6 outcomes with meaningful confidence across most protocols.
• HEALING series shows strongest aggregate performance (mean 6.98, std 1.77) with 12 protocols reaching strong or meaningful confidence.
• THRIVING series (mean 6.96, std 1.72) peaks at T-FC-L3 (7.63), suggesting cognitive optimization is reliably induced.
• TRANSFORMING series carries expected higher variance (std 1.87) due to ascending-arc design, but X-MC-L3 (5.7) underperforms beyond design expectations.
• YouTube reach remains micro-scale (<30 views for all protocols) — most efficacy signals are undiscovered by potential listeners.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series differentiation aligns with design intent, though TRANSFORMING shows more underperformance than expected.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols achieve tight clustering (std 1.55-1.93) with crisis protocols modestly lower as structurally anticipated.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols show upward trajectory by level, particularly in Focus & Clarity where L3 outperforms L1 by over one point.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols carry widest spread (std up to 2.45), consistent with low-affect targeting, but X-MC-L3 drops below acceptable range.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Three protocols deviate materially from expected patterns and warrants investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean 5.7, std 2.45) shows the lowest outcome in the library — high variance and isolated drift signal suggest listener-protocol mismatch.
• X-ME-L1 shows the largest AutoGluon delta (predicted 8.289 vs observed 6.74), indicating potential entrainment failure or population skew.
• Multiple protocols (H-AX-L1, T-FC-L3, H-OS-L1) show strong efficacy but negligible YouTube reach, representing discovery gaps rather than efficacy failures.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Multiple actionable signals emerge across the library.
• H-OS-L1 (n=95): highest n and strong outcomes — test promotional boost given 29 YouTube views represents underserved demand.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10): lowest mean requires redesign or exclusion from low-affect routing until validated.
• T-FC-L3 (n=35): highest THRIVING score (7.63) with 71% positive — warrants L4 variant creation for cognitive escalation.
• H-SL-L2 (n=45): "too busy" rhythm feedback from 3/15 qual responses suggests groove simplification for caregiver population.
• X-ME-L1 (n=39): investigate why AutoGluon predicts high cross-bucket efficacy (8.289) but observed mean remains lower.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals notable instability across protocols.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 28/30 protocols show sim < 0.85, with X-MC-L2 (0.631) and X-MC-L3 (0.634) indicating significant analytical framing shift between runs.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: X-MC-L2 is most isolated (mean_sim 0.584), while T-MB-L2 clusters tightly with X-ME-L2 (0.902) suggesting shared mechanism.
• Confidence note: drift signals are based on two-timepoint pairs; low run history limits stability claims.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: This anxiety protocol lands well — most people feel noticeably calmer within about ten minutes, and they really like the music style. It's not widely discovered yet, but those who try it tend to feel better.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Helps with stress for most who try it. Some feel better quickly, others take a bit longer. The rhythm feels comfortable to many. Not reaching many people yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: A solid calming choice for anxiety, though a few didn't feel much change. The sound is liked by most, but some needed the full session to settle.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Works for crisis moments though results vary. About half find real benefit, with relief often coming within five to ten minutes. Not well known yet but has strong potential.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Mixed reactions — some loved it, others found it just okay. Over half feel moderate improvement. Could work better with rhythm refinement.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Some people settle very quickly in crisis. Many notice real relief despite small sample size. AutoGluon suggests it might be the best for crisis even if not yet proven.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: One of the most-used protocols, with two-thirds reporting it helps with overstimulation. Many settled within ten minutes and loved the music. It's doing well but could reach more people.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Brings noticeable relief from over-stimulation for most, often within ten minutes. Some find the music just okay while others love it. Low viewership but solid results.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Popular for sleep, with many reporting it helps within five to ten minutes. The rhythm feels balanced though the music divides opinions. Not many have tried it yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Helps many relax into sleep quickly. Listeners enjoy the rhythm and sound. It's not seen much but works well for those who find it. Analytical models show some shifting patterns recently.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Works for sleep, though some caregivers found the rhythm slightly too active. Generally brings improvement within about ten minutes. Low visibility.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Good sleep results, often kicking in around five minutes. Some find the rhythm slightly busy but still effective for rest. Very few have tried it.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: About half report some creative boost. Many enjoy the music, with some loving it. Works quickly for some within the first minutes. One of the less effective in the set.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Those who try it mostly find creative benefit, often loving the musical style. Seems to pair well with focus protocols analytically. Not widely used yet.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Early data shows this might be the strongest for creative flow in the series, with most reporting improvement. Worth exploring further despite small sample.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Brings noticeable focus improvement to many, though some find the rhythm slightly busy. Better results may come from longer sessions or smoother flow.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Effective for focus, with significant improvement reported by many. Links closely to creative flow protocols in how it works. Not standout but solid.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: The standout for focus — over 70% report real improvement, often significant. Many love both the music and rhythmic qualities. Highest performing in THRIVING.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Good balance maintenance for most, used often in therapy contexts. Opinions split on the music — either liked or just neutral. Takes about twenty minutes to fully settle.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Helps maintain balance for many, though models suggest it might work even better than current scores show. May share qualities with mood protocols unexpectedly.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Early data looks promising for balance, though some don't fully settle with it. Worth trying when needing maintenance support. Mixed opinions on the rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Improves mind clarity for many, with strong music appeal. However, a couple report it makes things worse — needs targeted use for right listener type.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Shows potential for clarity but behaves differently than other protocols analytics-wise. Highest analytical instability — might work very well for specific listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Early results vary widely — works well for some but not all clarity needs. The highest level in this set may need redesign or validation. Lowest scoring protocol.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Some find significant motivational boost, though less than half report clear help. Models predict this should work much better — possible entrainment timing issue.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Limited data shows mixed motivation results. Some find long settle times — about twenty minutes. Shares hidden qualities with mood protocols that hint at design timing mismatch.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Shows good motivation potential despite small numbers. The highest level in this tier performs better than others, though sample is limited.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Models rate this as potentially the strongest protocol overall, though current scores don't quite match. Many love the music and find some energy benefits. Design may be underdelivering.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Good mood and energy results, with significant improvement for many. Works similarly to some maintenance protocols unexpectedly. More data would help confirm.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Early mood results are okay but not standout. Some find no effect while others report real energy lift — needs more data to understand the split.
